description.p: Emulate a wide range of Amiga models, from the classic A500 to the powerful A4000. Play thousands of games, run productivity software, or explore the demoscene — all with accurate hardware emulation and modern conveniences like save states, shader effects, and gamepad support.

Features include a built-in game launcher for easy one-click play, customizable controls, support for multiple disk image formats, and a full-featured settings interface for fine-tuning every aspect of the emulated hardware.

screenshot.caption: Amiberry running a classic A500 configuration

description.p: Feature release: multi-monitor RTG rendering, .uaem sidecar filesystem metadata, native OS file dialogs, multi-mouse support, autocrop and scaling improvements, HiDPI text rendering, and numerous bug fixes.
Major release: migrated from SDL2 to SDL3, new ImGui-based GUI, multi-pass GLSL shader presets, tablet pressure support, aspect ratio auto integer scaling, and numerous GUI panel improvements.
Bugfix release: CD Drive controller support, RISC-V build support, virtual keyboard fixes, NTSC auto-crop and scaling improvements, RAmiga key mapping option.
Added FreeBSD support and made build dependencies optional for broader platform compatibility.
Fixed keyboard navigation in GUI text fields, improved hard drive file handling on macOS, and added a Swap Backslash/F11 option.
Fixed GUI Expansion panel layout, network socket crashes, and filesystem handling. Added support for Kickstart 3.2.3 ROM files.
Fixed display crash with multiple monitors, improved GUI icons and status LED indicators.
Added GreaseWeazle drive cable options and improved display rendering stability.
Fixed serial port support, CD32 block caching, and improved debugger window focus handling.
Fixed crashes on macOS Intel systems.
Made external libraries optional for easier building, fixed ROM list refresh, and improved CD sector caching.
Harmonized keyboard mappings, improved file extension handling, and refined startup directory configuration.
Fixed TCP serial port connections.
Major release with installable packages for all platforms, a redesigned Expansions panel, PPC and network emulation support, serial port fixes, GUI themes, Hi-DPI support, and integer scaling.
Fixed sound panel controls, logfile naming, joystick input with RetroArch mapping, and a memory handling issue.
Added integer scaling, resolution autoswitch, and automatic MT-32 detection for WHDLoad titles.
Fixed custom options from config files being overwritten by WHDLoad XML settings, and a crash when saving configurations.
Added GUI theme foreground color option, folder creation button in file dialogs, and updated FloppyBridge library.
Added disk swapper events, default archive path for game files, system default audio device option, and a new WHDLoad settings panel.
Fixed memory mapping for expanded hardware, warp mode on-the-fly changes, and chipset configuration dropdown in the GUI.
Fixed CPU detection for AGA and CD32 games in the game launcher, and restored FPS counter display.
Fixed disk handling checks, 68030 MMU exception handling, memory boundary crossing, and game launcher CPU defaults.
Improved GUI layout with volume sliders, MIDI controls, MMU options, and a redesigned interface.
Preview release introducing the new graphical interface built with Dear ImGui.
First preview release of the redesigned emulator with a new graphical interface and major emulation core updates.
